response.justification: Candidate has strong overall software engineering experience (~15 years) and exposure to performance-sensitive systems via DLang, but the role specifically requires deep C++ expertise, industrial automation context, and IEC 62443 / SDL security practices. His only C++ experience is a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no evidence of industrial cybersecurity, MQTT, message brokers, or IoT/OT protocols. The technical center of gravity is firmly in web/Node/TypeScript and DLang, which does not match a Senior Manager C++ Digital Solutions role.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering with TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development discipline
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency, async services (Sociomantic) and event-driven architectures
Mentoring/teaching background — workshops, dojos, Selenium course — aligns with the mentorship responsibility
concerns: Effectively no recent C++/STL production experience; last C++ role was ~6 months in 2014–2015
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or related OT protocols
Cloud experience is AWS-centric; no Azure, and no UML or formal architecture documentation evidence

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and distributed-systems foundation
